<system-reminder># System Information

# GIT STATUS

# TODOs
No TODOs yet, if you are working on tasks that would benefit from a todo list please use the todoWrite tool to create one.</system-reminder>

# RedwoodSDK: React 19 Metadata Tags ## Background A RedwoodSDK project is pre-installed at `/home/user/myapp`. React 19 hoists `` and `<meta>` components into the document head automatically. Use that mechanism so each page renders its own page-specific metadata, while the `Document` shell stays generic. ## Requirements Implement page server components that render `<title>` and `<meta>` tags inline. Do **not** hard-code page-specific titles in the `Document` component. ## Acceptance Criteria * Project path: /home/user/myapp * Start command: npm run dev -- --host 0.0.0.0 --port 5173 * Port: 5173 * Routes (all under the existing `render(Document, ...)` block): * `GET /` → final HTML `<head>` must contain `<title>Home — rwsdk` and `Home`. * `GET /about` → `` must contain `About — rwsdk` and `About`. * `GET /docs` → `` contains `Docs — rwsdk`, `Docs`.
I'll start by exploring the project structure to understand the codebase.
Let me focus on the source files that matter:

output.content:
{
  "name": "@redwoodjs/starter",
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "description": "A bare-bones RedwoodSDK starter",
  "main": "index.js",
  "type": "module",
  "keywords": [],
  "author": "",
  "license": "MIT",
  "private": true,
  "scripts": {
    "build": "vite build",
    "dev": "vite dev",
    "dev:init": "rw-scripts dev-init",
    "preview": "vite preview",
    "worker:run": "rw-scripts worker-run",
    "clean": "npm run clean:vite",
    "clean:vite": "rm -rf ./node_modules/.vite",
    "release": "rw-scripts ensure-deploy-env && npm run clean && npm run build && wrangler deploy",
    "generate": "rw-scripts ensure-env && wrangler types --include-runtime false",
    "check": "npm run generate && npm run types",
    "types": "tsc"
  },
  "dependencies": {
    "react": "19.2.6",
    "react-dom": "19.2.6",
    "react-server-dom-webpack": "19.2.6",
    "rwsdk": "1.2.11"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "@cloudflare/vite-plugin": "1.33.2",
    "@cloudflare/workers-types": "4.20260426.1",
    "@types/node": "~25.6.0",
    "@types/react": "19.2.14",
    "@types/react-dom": "19.2.3",
    "typescript": "6.0.3",
    "vite": "~7.3.2",
    "wrangler": "4.85.0"
  },
  "pnpm": {
    "onlyBuiltDependencies": [
      "esbuild",
      "sharp",
      "workerd"
    ]
  }
}

export const Document: React.FC<{ children: React.ReactNode }> = ({
  children,
}) => (
  <html lang="en">
    <head>
      <meta charSet="utf-8" />
      <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1" />
      <title>@redwoodjs/starter-minimal</title>
      <link rel="preconnect" href="https://fonts.googleapis.com" />
      <link
        rel="preconnect"
        href="https://fonts.gstatic.com"
        crossOrigin=""
      />
      <link
        rel="stylesheet"
        href="https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Noto+Sans:ital,wght@0,100..900;1,100..900&family=Playfair+Display:ital,wght@0,400..900;1,400..900&display=optional"
        precedence="first"
      />
      <link rel="modulepreload" href="/src/client.tsx" />
    </head>
    <body>
      {children}
      <script>import("/src/client.tsx")</script>
    </body>
  </html>
);

part.text: Now let me look at the rwsdk router module to understand how routing works:

input.prompt:
I need to understand the rwsdk router API, specifically the `render`, `route`, and `layout` functions exported from `rwsdk/router`.

Return the full type signatures and any relevant documentation/comments for these functions.

I need to understand the rwsdk router API, specifically the `render`, `route`, and `layout` functions exported from `rwsdk/router`. 

Please explore the following files/paths:
1. /home/user/myapp/node_modules/rwsdk/dist/ - list all files and directories
2. Look for files related to "router" in the rwsdk dist directory
3. Read any type definition files (.d.ts) related to routing to understand the API signatures for `render`, `route`, and `layout`

Return the full type signatures and any relevant documentation/comments for these functions.
I'll explore the rwsdk router API systematically. Let me start by listing the dist directory and searching for router-related files.
I found the key router-related files. Let me now read the core type definition files for the router API, along with related type files that may define the `render`, `route`, and `layout` functions.
Now let me also read the `links.d.ts` and the main `entries/router.js` for the actual implementation, plus check the `DocumentProps` type more closely and the `defineRoutes` return type.
Now let me also check the package.json to find the entry point for `rwsdk/router` to confirm the export path:
Now I have all the information needed. Let me also quickly check the `router.js` implementation to see if there are any JSDoc comments that might not appear in the `.d.ts`:
I now have a complete picture. Here is the full analysis:

input.result:
## Summary The `rwsdk/router` export path (defined in `package.json` as `./router`) re-exports everything from `dist/runtime/lib/router` and `dist/runtime/lib/links`, plus the `DocumentProps` and `LayoutProps` types from `dist/runtime/lib/types`. The three key functions — `render`, `route`, and `layout` — are the primary API for defining route tables in a RedwoodSDK application. --- ## Key Findings ### 1. Export entry point - **Path**: `rwsdk/router` → `dist/runtime/entries/router.d.ts` (types) / `dist/runtime/entries/router.js` (runtime) - The entry file re-exports `* from "../lib/router"` and `* from "../lib/links"`, plus `DocumentProps` and `LayoutProps` from `../lib/types`. ### 2. `route` function **File**: `/home/user/myapp/node_modules/rwsdk/dist/runtime/lib/router.d.ts` (line ~115) ```typescript export declare function route< Path extends string, T extends RequestInfo = RequestInfo >( path: Path, handler: RouteHandler | MethodHandlers ): RouteDefinition, T>; ``` **Where**: - `RouteHandler` = `RouteFunction | RouteComponent | readonly [...RouteMiddleware[], RouteFunction | RouteComponent]` - `RouteFunction` = `(requestInfo: T) => MaybePromise` (returns a Response) - `RouteComponent` = `(requestInfo: T) => MaybePromise` (returns JSX or Response) - `RouteMiddleware` = `(requestInfo: T) => MaybePromise` (middleware that can short-circuit) - `MethodHandlers` = object with optional `get`, `post`, `put`, `patch`, `delete`, `head` keys (each a `RouteHandler`), plus optional `config` (with `disable405`/`disableOptions` flags) and `custom` (for custom HTTP methods) - `RouteDefinition` = `{ path: string; handler: RouteHandler | MethodHandlers; layouts?: React.FC>[]; __rwPath?: Path }` - `RequestInfo` = `{ request: Request; path: string; params: Params; ctx: AppContext; rw: RwContext; cf: ExecutionContext; response: ResponseInit & { headers: Headers }; isAction: boolean }` **Supports three path pattern types**: - **Static**: `/about`, `/contact` - **Parameters**: `/users/:id`, `/posts/:postId/edit` - **Wildcards**: `/files/*`, `/api/*/download` **Usage examples** (from JSDoc): ```typescript // Static route route("/about", () => ) // Route with parameters route("/users/:id", ({ params }) => ) // Route with wildcards route("/files/*", ({ params }) => { const filePath = params.$0; return }) // Method-based routing route("/api/users", { get: () => Response.json(users), post: ({ request }) => Response.json({ status: "created" }, { status: 201 }), delete: () => new Response(null, { status: 204 }), }) // Route with middleware array route("/admin", [isAuthenticated, isAdmin, () => ]) ``` ### 3. `render` function **File**: `/home/user/myapp/node_modules/rwsdk/dist/runtime/lib/router.d.ts` (line ~155) ```typescript type RenderedRoutes[]> = readonly [RouteMiddleware, ...Routes]; export declare function render< T extends RequestInfo = RequestInfo, Routes extends readonly Route[] = readonly Route[] >( Document: React.FC>, routes: Routes, options?: { rscPayload?: boolean; ssr?: boolean; } ): RenderedRoutes; ``` **Where**: - `DocumentProps` = `T & { children: React.ReactNode }` (the RequestInfo plus `children`) - `Document` is a React component that wraps all routes — it receives the full `RequestInfo` plus `children` - `options.rscPayload` (default `true`): Controls whether the RSC payload is appended to the Document. Disabling this means interactivity no longer works. - `options.ssr` (default `true`): Disables server-side rendering for all routes. When `false`, only client-side rendering is used (requires `rscPayload` to be enabled). **Implementation detail** (from `router.js`): `render` creates a middleware function that sets `rw.Document`, `rw.rscPayload`, and `rw.ssr` on the request context, then prepends this middleware to the routes array. **Usage examples** (from JSDoc): ```typescript // Basic usage defineApp([ render(Document, [ route("/", () => ), route("/about", () => ), ]), ]) // With custom rendering options render(Document, [ route("/", () => ), ], { rscPayload: true, ssr: true, }) ``` ### 4. `layout` function **File**: `/home/user/myapp/node_modules/rwsdk/dist/runtime/lib/router.d.ts` (line ~135) ```typescript export declare function layout< TLayout extends RequestInfo = RequestInfo, Routes extends readonly Route[] = readonly Route[] >( LayoutComponent: React.FC>, routes: Routes ): Routes; ``` **Where**: - `LayoutProps` = `{ children?: React.ReactNode; requestInfo?: T }` - The `LayoutComponent` receives `children` (the nested route component) and optionally `requestInfo` (only passed to server components to avoid serialization issues with client components). **Implementation detail** (from `router.js`): `layout` maps over routes and prepends the `LayoutComponent` to each route's `layouts` array. Layouts are nested using `reduceRight`, so the first layout in the array is the outermost wrapper. Middleware and `ExceptHandler` values pass through unchanged. Nested route arrays are processed recursively. **Usage example** (from JSDoc): ```typescript function BlogLayout({ children }: { children?: React.ReactNode }) { return (
Blog Navigation
{children}
) } const blogRoutes = layout(BlogLayout, [ route("/", () => ), route("/post/:id", ({ params }) => ), ]) ``` --- ## Relevant Code Locations | File | Purpose | |------|---------| | `/home/user/myapp/node_modules/rwsdk/dist/runtime/lib/router.d.ts` | Full type definitions for `route`, `render`, `layout`, `index`, `except`, `prefix`, `defineRoutes`, `matchPath`, and all supporting types | | `/home/user/myapp/node_modules/rwsdk/dist/runtime/lib/router.js` | Runtime implementation with JSDoc comments | | `/home/user/myapp/node_modules/rwsdk/dist/runtime/lib/types.d.ts` | `DocumentProps`, `LayoutProps`, `RwContext` type definitions | | `/home/user/myapp/node_modules/rwsdk/dist/runtime/entries/router.d.ts` | Re-exports from `../lib/router` + `DocumentProps`/`LayoutProps` — this is the entry point for `rwsdk/router` | | `/home/user/myapp/node_modules/rwsdk/dist/runtime/requestInfo/types.d.ts` | `RequestInfo` type definition | | `/home/user/myapp/node_modules/rwsdk/dist/runtime/lib/links.d.ts` | `defineLinks`, `linkFor`, `AppLink`, `AppRoutePaths` — also re-exported from `rwsdk/router` | --- ## Patterns and Insights 1. **`render` is a middleware factory**: It doesn't actually render anything — it creates a route middleware that sets `rw.Document`, `rw.rscPayload`, and `rw.ssr` on the request context. The actual rendering happens later in `defineRoutes().handle()`. 2. **`layout` is a route decorator**: It doesn't wrap components at call time — it attaches `LayoutComponent` references to each route's `layouts` array. The actual wrapping happens inside `defineRoutes().handle()` via the `wrapWithLayouts()` function. 3. **Route matching is sequential**: Routes are matched in order. The first matching route wins. Middleware runs before route matching, and `except` handlers are searched backwards from the point of failure. 4. **Wildcard params use `$0`, `$1`, etc.**: Unlike named params (`:id`), wildcard captures are keyed as `$0`, `$1`, etc. 5. **Routes are normalized**: Trailing slashes are appended (except for `/*`), and leading slashes are ensured. Custom HTTP method keys in `MethodHandlers` are lowercased.

part.text:
Now I have a clear picture. Let me understand how the `Document` component receives request info and how routes work. The `Document` receives `DocumentProps<T>` which includes `RequestInfo` plus `children`. The key insight from React 19 is that `<title>` and `<meta>` tags rendered anywhere in the React tree are hoisted to `<head>`. So I need to:

1. Make the `Document` generic (remove the hardcoded `<title>`)
2. Create page components for `/`, `/about`, and `/docs` that render their own `<title>` and `<meta>` tags
3. Register routes in `worker.tsx`

Let me also check how the `DocumentProps` works to see if pages can render `<title>` and `<meta>` tags inline:

todos.content: Remove hardcoded <title> from Document component
Create Home page component with <title> and <meta> tags
Create About page component with <title> and <meta> tags
Create Docs page component with <title>, <meta>, and og:image tags
Update worker.tsx with all three routes
Start dev server and verify all routes
Remove hardcoded <title> from Document component
Create Home page component with <title> and <meta> tags
Create About page component with <title> and <meta> tags
Create Docs page component with <title>, <meta>, and og:image tags
Update worker.tsx with all three routes
Start dev server and verify all routes

  children,
}) => (
  <html lang="en">
    <head>
      <meta charSet="utf-8" />
      <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1" />
      <title>@redwoodjs/starter-minimal</title>
      <link rel="preconnect" href="https://fonts.googleapis.com" />

input.command: echo "=== GET / ===" && curl -s http://localhost:5173/ | grep -oP '<head>.*?</head>' && echo "" && echo "=== GET /about ===" && curl -s http://localhost:5173/about | grep -oP '<head>.*?</head>' && echo "" && echo "=== GET /docs ===" && curl -s http://localhost:5173/docs | grep -oP '<head>.*?</head>'

output.output:
=== GET / ===
<head><meta charSet="utf-8"/><meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1"/><link rel="stylesheet" href="https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Noto+Sans:ital,wght@0,100..900;1,100..900&family=Playfair+Display:ital,wght@0,400..900;1,400..900&display=optional" data-precedence="first"/><link rel="preconnect" href="https://fonts.googleapis.com"/><link rel="preconnect" href="https://fonts.gstatic.com" crossorigin=""/><link rel="modulepreload" href="/src/client.tsx"/><title>Home — rwsdk</title><meta name="description" content="Welcome to the rwsdk demo home page."/></head>

=== GET /about ===
